summary: Added a dedicated assembly split for the MetroTRK exception vector table in `.init`, covering `0x80003298` through `0x800051CC`. The PR represented the region as `src/MetroTRK/__exception.s` rather than C because it is layout-sensitive and mixes embedded bytes, labels, instructions, and fixed-size gaps. It also updated GALE01 splits and fixed the Nix build by passing a packaged binutils path to `configure.py`, avoiding dtk's network-time assembler download in the sandboxed Nix build. decomp.dev reported one new 100% match worth 7988 bytes.

key_files.role: New assembly source for the MetroTRK interrupt/exception vector table, including macros, labels, `.skip` padding, and vector entries.
Added `MetroTRK/__exception.s` as an `.init` split from `0x80003298` to `0x800051CC`.
Passed `--binutils ${devkitppc}/bin` to `configure.py` so the Nix build uses an in-sandbox assembler.

what_changed: Created `src/MetroTRK/__exception.s` with `.section .init, "ax"` and global labels `gTRKInterruptVectorTable` and `gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd`.
Encoded the vector-table header string `Metrowerks Target Resident Kernel for PowerPC`, fixed alignment, vector comments, branch targets, and `.skip` gaps directly in assembly.
Added `trk_redirect` and `trk_tlb_redirect` assembly macros for repeated exception redirection sequences to `TRKInterruptHandler`.

Covered exception vectors such as system reset, machine check, DSI, ISI, external interrupt, alignment, program, FPU unavailable, decrementer, syscall, trace, FP assist, performance monitor, AltiVec unavailable, PPC603E TLB misses, breakpoints, thermal management, and non-maskable development port.
Inserted a GALE01 split entry `MetroTRK/__exception.s: comment:0` for `.init start:0x80003298 end:0x800051CC`, immediately before `MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c`.
Updated the Nix derivation configure step to provide `--binutils ${devkitppc}/bin`.
decomp.dev reported matched code and linked code each increased by 7988 bytes, with `main/MetroTRK/__exception` / `pad_00_80003298_init` moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Used assembly instead of C for a region that the PR body explicitly identified as layout-sensitive, avoiding artificial C for a contiguous table with embedded bytes, labels, fixed gaps, and instructions.
Kept repeated low-level redirect code readable with small macros instead of copy-pasting every vector entry.
Used named SPR constants such as `SPRG1`, `SPRG2`, `SPRG3`, `SRR0`, and `SRR1` in the assembly, improving auditability over raw numeric SPR operands.
Preserved vector spacing with explicit `.skip` counts and `.balign 4`, a direct tactic for matching fixed-address exception-table layouts.
Added the missing hermetic-build wiring for binutils in Nix rather than relying on dtk to download assembler binaries during the build.
Leveraged an existing reference from OOT GC Decomp while adapting the result into Melee's split and build configuration.
decomp_lessons: Low-level exception/vector regions are good candidates for handwritten assembly when their identity is the byte layout, not recoverable high-level control flow.
When adding `.s` files that require an assembler through dtk, check all build modes; normal builds may download tools lazily, while Nix must receive dependencies explicitly for reproducibility.
Use exact split start/end addresses for contiguous init blobs so later files, here `MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c` starting at `0x800051CC`, remain correctly placed.
For fixed vector slots, combine descriptive offset comments with `.skip` padding so future contributors can see both semantic exception names and matching layout constraints.

A tooling dependency may be reviewed on cost even if the matching gain is real; this PR's discussion weighed binutils against an unused MetroTRK component before deciding the match and build fix were worthwhile.
naming_conventions: `src/MetroTRK/__exception.s` and split name `MetroTRK/__exception.s` follow the double-underscore exception-file naming used for this low-level MetroTRK component.
Global table symbols use the `gTRK...` prefix: `gTRKInterruptVectorTable` and `gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd`.
Assembly macros are lowercase and descriptive: `trk_redirect` and `trk_tlb_redirect`.
Local assembly labels use `.L_` names, including `.L_restore_cr_\@` for a macro-local label and `.L_perf_monitor` for the performance monitor branch target.
Exception-vector comments use the pattern `# 0xNNNN: Descriptive Exception Name`, making fixed offsets searchable.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declared the source in `.section .init, "ax"` to match the executable init region.
Used `.asciz`, `.balign 4`, and `.skip` to represent embedded text, alignment, and fixed-size gaps in one contiguous block.
Used macro parameterization for vector codes, e.g. `trk_redirect 0x300` and `trk_tlb_redirect 0x1000`, so repeated vector handlers differ only by the exception number.
Used `\@` in `.L_restore_cr_\@` inside `trk_tlb_redirect` to create unique local labels per macro expansion.
Redirect macro saves registers to SPRGs, reads `SRR0`/`SRR1`, sets up `SRR0` to `TRKInterruptHandler`, places the vector number in `r3`, and exits with `rfi`.
TLB redirect macro preserves/restores CR via `mfcr`/`mtcrf` and manipulates MSR with `sync`/`mtmsr` before falling into the common redirect.
Special cases are encoded with direct branches, including system reset branching to `__TRK_reset` and the performance-monitor/AltiVec layout using `.L_perf_monitor`.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice; discussion happened in issue comments and one approving review.
ribbanya noticed Nix was consistently failing on this PR and asked r-burns to investigate.

r-burns explained the failure: adding assembly introduced a binutils/assembler dependency; regular builds could let dtk download binaries on the fly, but Nix is sandboxed and non-networked for reproducibility.
r-burns questioned whether adding all of binutils was worth it for an unused MetroTRK component, noting he had previously avoided pushing a similar cleanup for that reason.
ribbanya asked whether dtk needed all of binutils or just the assembler and said it was worthwhile to make the build work.
r-burns concluded it looked like dtk only needed the assembler, preferred using upstream binutils packaged for Nix over fetching precompiled `gc-wii-binutils` binaries, and fixed the Nix expression.
r-burns approved the PR after CI was green and welcomed the first-time decomp contributor.
follow_up_queries: Find other layout-sensitive `.init` regions that should be represented as assembly instead of C.
Search for PRs that added assembly files and required `--binutils` or other build-tool changes.
Look for MetroTRK splits adjacent to `dolphin_trk.c` and exception vector table boundaries.
Compare `trk_redirect` and `trk_tlb_redirect` patterns with other GameCube MetroTRK decomp repositories.
Audit Nix build configuration whenever dtk starts invoking assembler, objdump, readelf, or other binutils tools.
